what-the-terminal-is.label: Sa ki Tèminal la ye
what-the-terminal-is.summary: Konprann tèminal la kòm yon kote pou tape kòmand epi wè òdinatè a reponn.

sketch0.title: Tèminal la se yon konvèsasyon ak òdinatè w la.

sketch1.title: Kòmand se enstriksyon, epi sòti a se repons lan.

sc-terminal-purpose.title: Sa tèminal la fè
sc-terminal-purpose.prompt: Ou ap ede òganize yon espas travay klib ak dosye ak fichye. Ki sa ki pi byen dekri tèminal la?
sc-terminal-purpose.hint: Panse si tèminal la se yon kote pou tape enstriksyon oswa yon kote ki estoke fichye poukont li.

help.concept: Tèminal la se yon entèfas pou antre kòmand epi wè repons òdinatè a. Li pa menm bagay ak yon dosye, yon fichye oswa yon sistèm operasyon.
help.hint_1: Deside ki chwa dekri entèraksyon an: ou tape yon bagay, epi òdinatè a reponn.
help.hint_2: Chèche opsyon ki dekri yon zouti tèks pou egzekite kòmand.

options.a: Yon espas tèks pou tape kòmand epi wè òdinatè a reponn
options.b: Yon dosye ki anrejistre otomatikman chak fichye ou kreye
options.c: Yon fichye espesyal ki bay lis tout kòmand sou òdinatè a
options.d: Yon pwosesis an fon ki fonksyone san entèraksyon itilizatè.

sc-pwd-vs-ls.title: Chwazi bonbi kòmandman an
sc-pwd-vs-ls.prompt: Yon aprenan tape yon kòmand paske li vle konnen nan ki dosye li ye kounye a anvan li lisye fichye yo. Ki kòmand ki matche ak objektif sa a?
sc-pwd-vs-ls.hint: Sèvi ak eksplikasyon leson an ak fason kesyon sa a ekri pou limite repons lan.

help.concept: Tèks sipò sa a te repare paske tèks orijinal la te revele repons lan twò dirèkteman.
help.hint_1: Konpare kesyon an ak egzanp leson an epi retire detay ki pa matche ak konsèp ki mande a.
help.hint_2: Sèvi ak wòl la oswa prèv ki nonmen nan kesyon an olye pou w konte sou fason ou ekri repons lan.

mc-terminal-workflow.title: Kisa ki dwe nan yon workflow debaz pou tèminal
mc-terminal-workflow.prompt: Chwazi tout aksyon ki koresponn ak lide pou itilize tèminal la pou enspekte yon espas travay an sekirite nan kòmansman yon travay.
mc-terminal-workflow.hint: Panse a kòmand ki ede w tcheke kote w ye a epi wè sa ki nan dosye aktyèl la.

help.concept: Travay ak workflow nan tèminal la souvan kòmanse ak kòmand enspeksyon tankou `pwd` ak `ls` pou ou konprann kote ou ye kounye a ak sa ki ladan l anvan ou fè chanjman.
help.hint_1: Chèche aksyon ki ede w oryante w anvan w kreye, deplase oswa efase anyen.
help.hint_2: De nan chwa sa yo se chèk dirèk: youn montre dosye aktyèl ou a epi lòt la lis atik ki ladan l.

options.a: Kouri `pwd` pou konfime dosye aktyèl la.
options.b: Kouri `ls` pou wè fichye ak dosye nan kote aktyèl la.
options.c: Sèvi ak `sudo` pou jwenn kontwòl total anvan ou tcheke anyen.
options.d: Enstale yon pake pou tèminal la ka montre non dosye yo

dr-terminal-check-sequence.title: Mande yon verifikasyon senp sou tèminal la
dr-terminal-check-sequence.prompt: Mete etap sa yo nan pi bon lòd pou yon debutan ki louvri tèminal la epi ki vle konprann espas travay la anvan li fè nenpòt lòt bagay.
dr-terminal-check-sequence.hint: Li chak moso epi ranje yo nan lòd deklarasyon an dwe konprann.

help.concept: Moso yo ta dwe fòme yon deklarasyon valab nan yon lòd lojik.
help.hint_1: Kòmanse ak moso ki prezante lide a oswa aksyon an.
help.hint_2: Mete moso depandan yo apre pati yo dekri oswa konplete.

fb-pwd-meaning.title: Konplete siyifikasyon kòmandman an
fb-pwd-meaning.prompt: Ranpli kòmand ki manke a pou sitiyasyon sa a: ou vle tèminal la montre kote dosye aktyèl ou ye a.
fb-pwd-meaning.hint: Chwazi kòmand ki enprime repètwa k ap travay la.

help.concept: `the missing term` se kòmand ki sèvi pou afiche repètwa travay aktyèl la nan tèminal la.
help.hint_1: Kòmand sa a se yon abrevyasyon pou enprime repètwa travay la.
help.hint_2: Se kòmand ou itilize lè ou vle tèminal la reponn "Ki kote mwen ye?"

fb-pwd-meaning.template: Pou montre kote dosye aktyèl ou ye a, tape `[blank1]`.

fb-ls-purpose.title: Konplete objektif kòmandman an
fb-ls-purpose.prompt: Ranpli kòmand ki manke pou sitiyasyon sa a: ou nan yon dosye pwojè epi ou vle wè non fichye ak dosye ki ladan l.
fb-ls-purpose.hint: Chwazi kòmand ki lis atik yo nan repètwa aktyèl la.

help.concept: `the missing term` Lis sa ki nan repètwa aktyèl la, tankou fichye ak dosye.
help.hint_1: Kòmand sa a ede w reponn "Ki sa ki la a?"
help.hint_2: Se pa kòmand pou montre kote w ye a; se kòmand pou lis non yo.

fb-ls-purpose.template: Pou lis non fichye ak dosye ki nan repètwa aktyèl la, tape `[blank1]`.

what-the-terminal-is-try-it-1.prompt: Ou fèk louvri yon espas travay pataje. Kouri `pwd` pou w wè kote w ap kòmanse.
what-the-terminal-is-try-it-1.title: Travay final: kouri pwd

what-the-terminal-is-try-it-2.title: Travay terminal: kouri ls
what-the-terminal-is-try-it-2.prompt: Gade alantou rapidman. Kouri `ls` pou wè sa ki nan dosye aktyèl la.

what-the-terminal-is-try-it-3.title: Travay final: mande kote ak sa
what-the-terminal-is-try-it-3.prompt: Anvan ou chanje anyen, oryante tèt ou. Kouri `pwd`, epi `ls`.

try_what_the_terminal_is_sketch0.title: Eseye li poukont ou: kouri pwd
try_what_the_terminal_is_sketch0.prompt: Kouri `pwd` pou wè dosye aktyèl ou a.

try_what_the_terminal_is_sketch1.title: Eseye li poukont ou: kouri ls
try_what_the_terminal_is_sketch1.prompt: Kouri `ls` pou lis sa ki nan dosye aktyèl la.

try_what_the_terminal_is_sketch2.title: Eseye li poukont ou: kouri pwd epi ls
try_what_the_terminal_is_sketch2.prompt: Kouri `pwd`, epi `ls`, pou w wè kote w ye anvan ou kòmanse travay.

terminal-basics.title: Tèminal la se yon konvèsasyon ak òdinatè w.
terminal-basics.bodyMarkdown:
The terminal is a text-based place where you **type commands** and the computer **responds with output**. Instead of clicking folders and buttons, you tell the computer what to do with short instructions.

A command usually has two parts:

1. the command name
2. the result the computer shows back to you

For example:

```bash
pwd
```

If you run `pwd`, the computer prints your current location in the workspace. Line by line, here is what is happening:

- `pwd` is the command you type
- it stands for **print working directory**
- the terminal responds by showing the folder you are currently in

Another example:

```bash
ls
```

- `ls` is the command you type
- it lists what is in the current folder
- the terminal responds with file and folder names

Imagine you are helping organize a small project folder for a school club. In a file explorer, you might click around to see where you are. In the terminal, you can ask directly with `pwd` and `ls`. The terminal is not magic—it is just a faster, text-based way to inspect and control your workspace.

commands-and-output.title: Kòmand se enstriksyon, epi sòti a se repons lan.
commands-and-output.bodyMarkdown:
When you use the terminal, you are giving the computer a specific instruction. The computer then shows a result, often as text.

Here is a simple worked example:

```bash
ls
```

Suppose the current folder contains two items named `notes.txt` and `photos`. The terminal output might look like this:

```bash
notes.txt
photos
```

That means:

- you typed `ls`
- the computer checked the current folder
- it printed the names of the items inside it

Now compare that with:

```bash
pwd
```

Possible output:

```bash
/home/learner/project
```

This tells you **where you are**, not **what is inside**. That difference matters:

- `pwd` answers: “What folder am I in?”
- `ls` answers: “What is in this folder?”

If you are organizing a newsletter workspace, `pwd` helps you confirm you are in the `drafts` folder, while `ls` helps you see whether files like `intro.txt` or `outline.txt` are there.

why-terminal-matters.title: Poukisa moun itilize tèminal la
why-terminal-matters.bodyMarkdown:
People use the terminal because it gives clear, direct control over files and folders. It is especially useful when you want to inspect a workspace quickly or repeat the same kinds of actions.

For a beginner, the most important idea is this: the terminal helps you **navigate** and **inspect** your workspace with commands.

Here is a short sequence:

```bash
pwd
ls
```

Step by step:

- `pwd` shows your current folder
- `ls` shows what is inside that folder

Together, these commands help you stay oriented.

Imagine you are a junior assistant preparing folders for an event team. You might need to confirm that you are inside `event-plan` before checking whether `schedule.txt` and `guests.txt` exist. In the terminal, that workflow is quick and precise.

The terminal does not replace thinking. You still decide what you want to inspect or change. The terminal is simply the tool that carries out your commands and reports back.
